content.method: Structured parent surveys with discrete-choice/conjoint methodology across regions and SES strata; administrative analysis of application patterns before/after known school-quality shocks; qualitative interviews in low-participation rural feeder schools to assess non-application barriers.
content.question: What is the actual and counterfactual distribution of parental demand for selective tracks across SES groups, regions, and ethnic/minority status, and how much of current enrollment growth reflects genuine pedagogical demand versus credentialing/signaling or escape from deteriorating general schools?
content.why_it_matters: This affects the political feasibility of any contraction or delay proposal (T2, T3, T6) and clarifies whether demand-side interventions (T1) or supply-side interventions (T2/T3/T6) are better targeted at the actual driver of stratification.
